name: Naive baseline
rationale: Establish the lowest-effort baseline: smallest model, no retrieval, default decoding. Defines the metric floor against which every later variant claims improvement.

name: Strong baseline from knowledge library
rationale: If the knowledge library has a pattern matching this capability+domain, materialize it as the starting variant. This is how cross-project knowledge compounds — first project on a fresh install skips this seed.

name: Bigger-model probe
rationale: Run the same prompt on a larger model (Sonnet or Opus) once to bound the headroom. If Sonnet doesn't beat Haiku here, the bottleneck is not model capacity — focus on prompt/retrieval/data.

name: Retrieval-augmented probe
rationale: For RAG-flavored missions only: add minimal retrieval (BM25 top-3) to the baseline. Determines whether the bottleneck is missing context vs reasoning.

name: Judge calibration probe
rationale: Run the judge against the gold-labeled calibration subset to bound judge trust. If judge agreement is <0.85, every subsequent judge_score win is suspect.

name: Few-shot seed
rationale: Add 3 in-context examples drawn from the eval set's easiest tag. Tests whether the bottleneck is prompt clarity vs model capacity.

name: Temperature probe
rationale: Bump temperature to 0.5 (from 0.0). For open-ended agents this often helps; for extraction it usually hurts. Cheap to test once.

name: Chunking probe
rationale: Halve chunk_size to 256 tokens with 32 overlap. Smaller chunks usually lift retrieval precision but can fragment context; worth probing once.
